page: Microsoft Azure D16pds_v6 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the Dpdsv6 family, powered by an ARM-based Microsoft Neoverse-N2 processor. This instance features 16 dedicated physical cores and 16 vCPUs running at 3.4 GHz, with a single thread per core. The hardware configuration includes 64.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core, alongside 945 GB of bundled local storage. It operates on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor and supports premium storage options.

Benchmark results indicate strong memory performance, with the Passmark memory score ranking in the top tier. Large-block read-only memory bandwidth reaches 312.21 GB/sec, and write bandwidth registers at 125.75 GB/sec, both placing in the top 25% of tested servers. Single-threaded gzip compression is also strong at 32.98 GB/sec. Standard computational benchmarks, such as Geekbench and stress-ng multi-core tests, fall within the average range. However, the server exhibits weaker performance in prompt processing for medium-sized 7B large language models, though text generation speeds remain average.

The ARM64 architecture of the D16pds_v6 offers high resource density and qualitative cost efficiency for workloads optimized for ARM instruction sets. The combination of dedicated physical cores, high-speed memory bandwidth, and substantial local storage makes this instance suitable for general-purpose workloads, web serving, database operations, and software compilation. It is less suited for heavy large language model inference or workloads requiring specialized x86 instruction sets like AVX-512.
description: Microsoft Azure D16pds_v6 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the Dpdsv6 family, powered by an ARM64 Microsoft Neoverse-N2 processor. It features 16 dedicated physical cores, 16 vCPUs running at 3.4 GHz, 64.0 GB of RAM, and 945 GB of local storage. Operating on Microsoft Hyper-V, this instance delivers top-tier memory performance, with large-block read bandwidth reaching 312.21 GB/sec and a Passmark memory score in the top 10%. While standard CPU benchmarks and multi-threaded decompression fall in the average range, single-threaded gzip compression is strong. The ARM-based architecture provides high resource density, making it a cost-efficient choice for general-purpose applications, database operations, web serving, and software compilation.

64.0 GB of system memory paired with 945 GB of bundled local storage
Top-tier memory performance with large-block read bandwidth of 312.21 GB/sec
